hypothesis: Integrating a dedicated molecular image detection and segmentation module—trained specifically on the heterogeneous layouts of chemical literature—into the single-agent preprocessing pipeline will yield a statistically significant improvement in SMILES extraction F1 scores across both nanomaterial and small molecule datasets in ChemX, compared to pipelines relying solely on text-based LLM extraction, because the near-zero SMILES extraction performance observed uniformly across all evaluated methods is attributable primarily to the failure to localize and segment molecular structure images rather than to LLM reasoning deficiencies.
